macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Inflation Slows to 3.5% in June, Below Expectations
items.summary: US inflation slowed to 3.5% in June, lower than the 3.8% analysts expected, driven by a break in gasoline prices.

items.title: Trump Administration Imposes 50% Tariffs on Canadian Goods
items.summary: The US imposed 50% tariffs on Canadian wine, hockey sticks, cement, and other products, citing discrimination and sparking fears of an escalating trade war.

items.title: Fed Expected to Hold Rates Steady Through 2026 Despite Inflation
items.summary: A Reuters poll of economists forecasts the Federal Reserve will keep interest rates steady for the rest of 2026 to address a five-year inflation problem, though some cite high chances of a hike.

items.title: US Stock Futures Rise on Iran Ceasefire Hopes and Earnings Focus
items.summary: US stock index futures gained on Tuesday as renewed Middle East mediation efforts lifted sentiment, with investors awaiting pivotal corporate earnings for the AI market.

items.title: S&P 500 and Oil Indices Show Minor Declines Amid Geopolitical Volatility
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 0.19% while crude oil and petroleum indices dropped 0.41% as oil prices dipped due to mediation efforts offsetting US-Iran strikes.

items.title: SpaceX Announces Maiden Earnings Report Date, Stock Gains
items.summary: SpaceX stock gained on Tuesday, potentially snapping a seven-day losing streak, after announcing its maiden earnings report will be released on August 4.

items.title: Euro Zone Banks Tighten Credit Access Due to War Fears
items.summary: Euro zone banks tightened credit access in Q2 over geopolitical instability fears and expect further tightening, according to the European Central Bank's Bank Lending Survey.

items.title: Hungary Central Bank Cuts Base Rate to 5.75%
items.summary: Hungary's central bank cut its base rate to 5.75% and signaled more rate cuts through the summer.

items.title: Major US Companies Beat Earnings Expectations
items.summary: General Motors, Northrop Grumman, 3M, and Marsh & McLennan all reported earnings above expectations, with GM at $3.57 and Northrop Grumman at $7.68.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $66,000 as Crypto Stocks Rally
items.summary: Bitcoin jumped nearly 3% in 24 hours to $66,886, its highest level in over a month, lifting crypto stocks like Coinbase (up 11%) and Marathon Digital (up 6%) amid optimism for the CLARITY Act.

items.title: European Authorities Seize $1.51B Bitcoin-Mixing Service Cryptomixer
items.summary: Europol dismantled the crypto-mixing platform Cryptomixer, used by ransomware groups to launder bitcoin, seizing servers and $29 million in BTC as part of a $1.51 billion operation.

items.title: Trump Agrees to Ethics Provision, Advancing US Crypto Bill
items.summary: President Trump agreed to ethics restrictions tied to sweeping US cryptocurrency legislation, removing a major obstacle and shifting the next decision to Senate Democrats ahead of a potential vote on the CLARITY Act.

items.title: SEC Charges Florida Man in $22 Million Crypto Mining Fraud
items.summary: The SEC charged a Florida man and his company in an alleged $22 million crypto mining fraud scheme, marking another significant regulatory enforcement action in the sector.

items.title: Polkadot Plunges 11% Breaking Below $2.05 Support
items.summary: Polkadot (DOT) collapsed 11% to $2.02 amid a broader market selloff, breaking below the $2.05 support level and exposing the psychological $2.00 threshold.

items.title: Dogecoin Drops 23% Amid US-Iran Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Dogecoin (DOGE) suffered a steep 23.1% decline to $0.072 on July 20, driven by heightened risk aversion as escalating US-Iran tensions curbed investor appetite.

items.title: Gleec Acquires Komodo’s Cross-Chain DeFi Stack in $23.5M Deal
items.summary: Gleec bought Komodo’s cross-chain DeFi stack, including atomic-swap technology and core developers, in a $23.5 million acquisition to expand its ecosystem.

items.title: Japan to Cut Crypto Tax Burden to 20% Uniform Rate
items.summary: Japan’s government proposed cutting the crypto tax burden to a uniform 20% rate, categorizing crypto profits under a separate-taxation framework to boost local Bitcoin trading.

items.title: Ripple Approved for Wider XRP and RLUSD Services in Singapore
items.summary: Singapore’s regulator approved Ripple to offer wider services for XRP and RLUSD, enabling the company to expand its payment and stablecoin operations in the region.

items.title: Visa Launches New Stablecoin Platform with Open USD
items.summary: Visa launched a new platform for stablecoins integrating Open USD, aiming to streamline digital payment infrastructure and expand stablecoin adoption.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Releases Three Cheaper Gemini AI Models; Pro Timing Still Unclear
items.summary: Alphabet's Google launched three new, more affordable versions of its Gemini AI model on Tuesday, but provided no launch date for the flagship Gemini 3.5 Pro, which has already faced delays.

items.title: Anthropic Secures $1.5B Copyright Settlement Approval in Landmark Authors Case
items.summary: A federal judge in San Francisco approved Anthropic's $1.5 billion settlement with a group of authors who accused the company of misusing their books to train its Claude chatbot, with only 350 authors opting out.

items.title: Chinese Startup Moonshot AI Halts Kimi K3 Subscriptions After Overwhelming Demand
items.summary: Moonshot AI temporarily stopped new subscriptions for its Kimi K3 model, a 2.8 trillion-parameter open-weight system claimed to be the world's largest, as demand pushed capacity near its limits.

items.title: Microsoft Invests Billions in Mistral's European Infrastructure and U.S. Distribution
items.summary: Microsoft agreed to spend billions on Mistral's computing infrastructure in Europe, a deal that will also expand the French AI startup's technology distribution through Microsoft's U.S. cloud and software platforms.

items.title: Humanoid Robotics Startup Raises $152M Series A at $1.35B Valuation
items.summary: UK-based robotics firm Humanoid secured $152 million in Series A funding, reaching a post-money valuation of $1.35 billion as investors increasingly support companies building robots to augment human labor.

items.title: U.S. and China Plan September AI Talks to Address Frontier Model Risks
items.summary: The United States and China are scheduled to hold talks on AI regulation in September, focusing on how to manage risks from increasingly powerful rival frontier models.

items.title: Thinking Machines Lab Launches First In-House Model 'Inkling' Co-Founded by Mira Murati
items.summary: Thinking Machines Lab, an AI startup co-founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, released its first in-house model named Inkling, a 975-billion-parameter system.

items.title: Alibaba Launches Open-Weight Qwen 3.8 Model with 24 Trillion Parameters
items.summary: Alibaba introduced its latest open-weight AI model, Qwen 3.8, featuring a massive 24 trillion parameters, marking a significant advancement in Chinese open-source AI development.

items.title: Data Centers Expected to Use Four Times More Electricity by 2035 Amid AI Boom
items.summary: Industry analysis projects that data centers fueling artificial intelligence will consume four times more electricity by 2035, highlighting growing energy infrastructure challenges.

items.title: New York Blocks New Large AI Data Centers for Up to One Year for Environmental Protection
items.summary: New York state announced a temporary ban on new large data centers that support AI operations for up to a year to protect the environment and stabilize the energy grid.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bethesda Confirms Fallout 3 and New Vegas Remasters Are in Development
items.summary: Bethesda officially announced in a new blog post that remasters of Fallout 3 and Fallout: New Vegas are currently in development, alongside confirmation that Fallout 5 is in preproduction and a new Obsidian-led Fallout game is confirmed.

items.title: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 Beta and NEXT Showcase Announced for August
items.summary: Activision revealed the upcoming Call of Duty: NEXT showcase on August 21st to debut live gameplay for Modern Warfare 4, which will also feature two beta weekends in late August.

items.title: Persona 4 Revival Remake Confirmed for February 2027 Release
items.summary: ATLUS released a new trailer for Persona 4 Revival highlighting Chie Satonaka and confirmed the remake will launch on February 18, 2027, across PS5, Xbox Series, and PC.

items.title: Game of Thrones: War for Westeros Delayed to Early 2027
items.summary: The highly anticipated Game of Thrones: War for Westeros, originally slated for a 2026 release, has been delayed and is now set to launch in early 2027.

items.title: Assassin's Creed: Black Flag Resynced Surpasses Three Million Sales
items.summary: Ubisoft's resynced version of Assassin's Creed: Black Flag has officially sold over three million copies, with the lead developer urging players to support the game when GTA 6 launches.

items.title: Midgar Studio Ceases Operations Following Edge of Eternity Success
items.summary: Midgar Studio, the developer known for Edge of Eternity and Edge of Memories, has officially ceased operations, marking a significant loss for the indie RPG community.

items.title: UK Game Store GAME Falls to Administration with £16M Debt
items.summary: Major UK video game retailer GAME has entered administration due to £16 million in debt, raising concerns about the future of physical game retail in the region.

items.title: Marathon Director Joe Ziegler Confirms Departure from Bungie
items.summary: Four months after the launch of Marathon, the game's director Joe Ziegler has confirmed he has quit Bungie, following the earlier exit of General Manager and new promotions within the studio.

items.title: Wreckreation 2 Announced with Community Collaboration
items.summary: The arcade racing sequel Wreckreation 2 has been announced for PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S, developed in collaboration with its community, though no release date is currently set.

items.title: Final Fantasy 14 Mobile Global Launch Cancelled
items.summary: Final Fantasy 14 Mobile is being shut down in China, and Square Enix has officially cancelled the game's global launch following the China shutdown.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Meta Adds Xbox Game Pass Starter to Meta Horizon+ Subscription
items.summary: Meta Platforms announced Tuesday it is adding the Xbox Game Pass starter edition to its Meta Horizon+ subscription service, expanding gaming offerings on Quest virtual reality headsets.

items.title: Apple Launches 'Apple Upgrade' Device Leasing Program on July 28
items.summary: Apple is launching a new device leasing program called 'Apple Upgrade' in the U.S. on July 28 to boost sales, according to Bloomberg News.

items.title: Garmin Launches $200 Screenless Cirqa Smart Band Fitness Tracker
items.summary: Garmin has launched the Cirqa Smart Band, a $200 screenless fitness tracker monitoring 80+ activities with up to 10 days of battery life, designed to compete with Whoop.

items.title: Samsung Rolls Out Beta of AI-Powered Health Assistant Chatbot
items.summary: Samsung's Health Assistant chatbot is now available in beta for eligible U.S. users, acting as a virtual health companion with new heart metrics tools.

items.title: Google Connects New Transatlantic Subsea Cable to Portugal
items.summary: Alphabet's Google successfully connected a new transatlantic subsea cable to Sines in Portugal, adding another U.S.-Europe data route amid surging cloud computing demand.

items.title: Nvidia Details Vera CPU for Data Centers with Custom Core Design
items.summary: Nvidia unveiled its Vera CPU, its first data center CPU with a custom core design featuring 88 cores and 176 threads, set for general release in the second half of 2026.

items.title: New York Imposes First Statewide Moratorium on Large Data Centers
items.summary: New York will block new large data centers fueling AI for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid, marking the country's first statewide moratorium.

items.title: University of Tennessee Sues Anthropic Over Neural Network Patents
items.summary: The research arm of the University of Tennessee sued Anthropic in Delaware federal court for allegedly infringing patents related to neural networks.
